name: Junbo He
title: Independent Builder / Product-minded Frontend Engineer
tagline: I design and ship calm, useful digital products where product thinking and frontend craft meet.
bio: I work at the intersection of frontend engineering, product design, and independent product building. I care about turning fuzzy ideas into clear user journeys, structured interfaces, and systems that are pleasant to maintain.

Most of my recent work lives around fitness, personal knowledge systems, and content-driven products. I enjoy the part where product framing, data structure, interface rhythm, and engineering quality all have to support each other.

focusAreas.title: Fitness x Product
focusAreas.description: Exploring how training data, habit loops, and progress visualization can become better personal products.

focusAreas.title: Content Systems
focusAreas.description: Designing websites and publishing flows where content stays structured and the UI remains easy to extend.

focusAreas.title: Calm Software
focusAreas.description: Building interfaces that feel thoughtful, readable, and low-noise without losing product depth.

now: Shaping a fitness logging product that makes muscle-group balance visible instead of hiding it inside raw workout tables.
Refining a personal site architecture that keeps content local-first today and ready for headless CMS tomorrow.
Writing more in public about the overlap between product taste, UI structure, and engineering quality.

timeline.title: Moved from shipping pages to thinking in product systems
timeline.description: Started caring less about isolated screens and more about content modeling, user flow, and maintainable frontend architecture.

timeline.title: Began building small tools for my own workflows
timeline.description: Created internal tools for notes, planning, and knowledge capture, which made product iteration feel much more practical.

timeline.title: Focused on health and training products
timeline.description: Turned personal training pain points into product ideas around consistency, progression, and visual feedback.

timeline.title: Building an independent product portfolio with stronger storytelling
timeline.description: Combining case-study thinking, cleaner frontend systems, and a more intentional personal brand presence.
